mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) across 100+ rate cut observations, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately trigger risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to predict direction with accuracy above 55% in backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, driving sustained positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 60-day returns in > 60% of cases, or interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects supply-side constraints that compress corporate margins and force negative S&P 500 returns over one month.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods yield positive 20-day returns in > 58% of observations, or the mechanism shows zero predictive power outside the specified regime.

mechanism_narrative: When core inflation falls below 2.0% while the federal funds rate remains above 2.0%, markets price in imminent policy easing, generating positive S&P 500 returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in > 60% of threshold-crossing events, or the inflation-rate spread loses predictive accuracy (Rank IC < 0.01) over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining nonfarm payrolls and rising unemployment indicates structural labor market deterioration that suppresses earnings expectations and drives negative equity returns over three months.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence fails to predict negative 60-day returns in > 55% of cases, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant when controlling for GDP growth.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth above 2.0% confirms robust aggregate demand that supports corporate revenue expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: GDPC1 values above 2.0% growth yield negative 20-day returns in > 60% of observations, or the signal out-of-sample Sharpe ratio falls below 0.5 over 5-year periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production alongside accelerating inflation creates stagflationary pressures that force immediate risk-off selling, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day returns remain positive in > 65% of stagflationary observations, or the combined supply-inflation signal shows no directional edge in walk-forward testing.
